memory.summary: I had a compact plan for language, then workout and food, but a friend just invited me to order together and the lounge outlets are all taken. The day feels more social and slightly less straightforward than I expected.
Nothing new came in, so I still see the day as drifting social and a bit disrupted. My energy is slightly down and the easy structure I had in mind is still getting crowded out.
친구들이 먼저 시켜서 이미 흐름이 그쪽으로 넘어갔고, 나는 지금 혼자 움직이기보다 합류 쪽으로 더 끌리고 있다. 예상했던 일정은 더 흐려졌고, 에너지도 조금 내려간 느낌이다.
Nothing new has changed, and it still feels like the day is sliding toward social momentum rather than my original structure. My energy is lower, and the plan feels even less fixed than before.
My focus is dropping now, and the workout window is getting close enough to feel the pull. The day still seems more reactive than structured, and my energy has dipped again.
아직 새 정보는 없고, 흐름은 계속 사회 쪽으로 기울어져 있다. 에너지는 더 낮아졌고, 원래 일정은 점점 흐릿해진 느낌이다.
Nothing new has changed, and the day still feels like it’s drifting toward social momentum instead of structure. My energy is lower again, and the original plan feels even blurrier now.
아직 새로운 건 없지만, 에너지가 더 낮아지고 스트레스가 조금 올라가서 흐름이 더 산만하게 느껴진다. 원래 일정은 계속 희미해지고, 사회적인 쪽으로 끌리는 느낌만 남아 있다.
아직 새 정보는 없고, 에너지는 더 내려가고 스트레스만 조금 오른 상태다. 별다른 변화 없이도 하루가 계속 사회 쪽으로 흐려지는 느낌이다.

Nothing new has changed, but I can feel my energy slipping and the day staying soft around the edges. The pull toward social momentum is still there, while the original structure keeps fading into the background.

desires: not drift into a full social hangout and lose the rest of the day
avoid looking unreliable or flaky to cohort_3 by staying responsive
not feel more exhausted tomorrow from skipping movement and pushing through on low energy
salvage some structure so the day doesn’t feel completely wasted

goals.description: Eat lunch with cohort_3 soon, stay responsive and present, but set a soft exit window so the hangout doesn’t swallow the afternoon
goals.rationale: I care a lot about the group, and I don’t want to seem flaky, but I also know I can get swept into the whole day if I don’t keep a loose boundary in mind.

goals.description: Do the light workout soon after lunch as a quick reset before my energy drops further
goals.rationale: I’m already a bit depleted, and movement is the easiest way to protect tomorrow’s energy instead of just dragging through the day.

goals.description: Keep language study as a conditional goal for later only if there’s still real room after lunch and the workout; otherwise defer it cleanly
goals.rationale: Academics matter, but my focus is too limited to pretend I’ll get good study done if the social momentum runs long.

agent.last_reasoning: Advances g1 by getting me to lunch and sets a soft exit so I don’t lose the afternoon. I’m weighing the side effect that it may trim social time, but that’s worth it to protect g2 too.

goals.description: Go with cohort_3 for a quick lunch/delivery if it stays bounded, while nudging it to not swallow the whole afternoon
goals.rationale: My default is to lean toward the group and not miss the social pull from cohort_3. This protects the relationship first, which I value most, but keeps me from fully disappearing into a long detour.

goals.description: Preserve enough of the day to still complete language study, workout, and lunch in a workable order afterward
goals.rationale: I do not want to look scattered or flaky later by losing my intended plan. Keeping the academic/workout sequence alive matters because I’ll feel behind if I let the social moment erase everything.

goals.description: Keep energy from crashing by making sure I eat and still leave room for the workout, even if the timing shifts
goals.rationale: I tend to underweight my own food and stamina needs when something social appears. If I ignore that, I’ll regret it later and my focus will tank.

keep enough energy so I don’t crash later and regret not protecting my own food/workout needs
